scenario: A Node.js application is setting up @camunda8/sdk to communicate with a SaaS cluster. The dev wants to know how the ZBClient (or equivalent) is created -- what config it needs.
question: How is the Node SDK client typically configured for SaaS?

options.text: The Camunda 8 TypeScript SDK does not auto-detect credentials from the environment without any explicit configuration being provided. While the SDK can read from environment variables as a convenience, those environment variables must be set with the correct cluster address, OAuth2 client credentials, and token endpoint values before the SDK can authenticate. Creating a client without any configuration would result in authentication failures because the SDK has no way to discover where the cluster is or what credentials to use.

Camunda 8 SaaS does not use username and password (basic authentication) for SDK authentication. The platform uses OAuth2 client credentials grant flow, which requires a client ID and client secret obtained from the Camunda Console when creating an API client. Username and password authentication is not an available option for programmatic SDK access to Camunda 8 SaaS clusters.

A simple API key is not the authentication model for Camunda 8 SaaS SDK access. The platform uses OAuth2 client credentials grant flow, which involves exchanging a client ID and client secret for a short-lived access token at the token endpoint. This access token is then used as a Bearer token in subsequent API and gRPC calls. This is more secure than static API keys because access tokens expire and are rotated automatically.

io for SaaS). The SDK handles OAuth2 flow (token fetch / refresh / caching) internally. Code: import { Camunda8 } from '@camunda8/sdk'; const c8 = new Camunda8({ ZEEBE_ADDRESS, ZEEBE_CLIENT_ID, ZEEBE_CLIENT_SECRET, CAMUNDA_OAUTH_URL });. md); Pattern #36 -- apis-tools/node-js-sdk/ is 404, real docs at apis-tools/typescript/.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 SaaS uses OAuth2 client-credentials grant for SDK authentication. SDK setup: 1. Console UI: provision an API Client → get client ID + client secret + audience URLs. 2. SDK config: pass cluster address, client credentials, and OAuth2 token endpoint. 3. SDK runtime: on first call, exchanges credentials for an access token at the OAuth2 endpoint; uses Bearer token in subsequent gRPC / REST calls; refreshes before expiry. Environment-variable convention (typical): ZEEBE_ADDRESS: cluster's Zeebe gRPC address (e.g., .zeebe.camunda.io:443). ZEEBE_CLIENT_ID: API client ID. ZEEBE_CLIENT_SECRET: API client secret. CAMUNDA_OAUTH_URL: Auth0 token endpoint. The SDK reads from environment by default, or accept explicit config object in code. For Self-Managed clusters: same OAuth2 model; just point to your IdP's token endpoint.
